methodsAttempted.query: site:usask.ca University of Saskatchewan generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching learning privacy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced official USask academic-integrity, teaching, library, CGPS, and Computer Science GenAI sources.
The second official-domain query confirmed the central academic-integrity GenAI page and department-scoped Computer Science graduate policy.
The root www.usask.ca robots.txt allows ordinary public pages and disallows only selected sandbox, test, and CGI paths.

methodsAttempted.query: ai.usask.ca guidelines student educator researcher administrative
methodsAttempted.notes: Direct official fetches verified the central Artificial Intelligence at USask page plus student, educator, researcher, and administrative guideline pages.

artifacts.summary: Official USask discovery found central AI principles and role-specific guidelines, academic-integrity GenAI guidance, teaching and learning GenAI tool guidance, and a CGPS graduate framework PDF. Department-only Computer Science guidance and a login-gated TeamDynamix KB candidate were documented but not used for university-wide claims.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Saskatchewan publishes central AI principles and role-specific AI guidelines for students, educators, researchers, and administrators.

artifacts.claimText: USask student AI guidance says students should use AI to support, not replace or misrepresent, their learning, follow instructor rules, and avoid inputting personal information or confidential data into AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Student Guidelines for AI Use - Artificial Intelligence at USask
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Student Guidelines for AI Use - Artificial Intelligence at USask
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Use AI to support your learning, not to replace or misrepresent your learning. Action: Follow the rules laid out by instructors about use of AI to act with integrity and avoid academic misconduct. It is strongly recommended that you do not input personal information or confidential data into an AI tool.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Student Guidelines for AI Use - Artificial Intelligence at USask
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow student-guidance claim; it does not assert a blanket ban or binding policy beyond the cited official guidance.

artifacts.claimText: USask educator AI guidance says educators should discuss AI-use expectations with learners and remain responsible for assessing student work, feedback quality, and grades.

citation.citationTitle: Educator Guidelines for Use of AI
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Educator Guidelines for Use of AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You should discuss expectations with learners about appropriate AI use and its impact on learning, including accountability for any use of AI output, and disclosing AI use. You are responsible for assessing student work, feedback quality, and establishing student grades, although you can be supported by AI.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Change and Innovation in Instruction and Assessment section
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Educator Guidelines for Use of AI
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as teaching guidance. The wording keeps 'should' because the source itself uses should-language.

artifacts.claimText: USask research AI guidance says researchers are accountable for output integrity and should consider tool security, protect confidential or sensitive information, and be transparent about AI use.

citation.citationTitle: Research, Scholarly, and Artistic Work Guidelines for Use of AI
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Research, Scholarly, and Artistic Work Guidelines for Use of AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Researchers recognize that data input into AI data processers may be accessed by others resulting in privacy breaches and/or disclosure of confidential information. Researchers consider the security of AI tools to prevent the leakage, accidental or otherwise, of confidential, proprietary, or sensitive information. Researchers should be transparent about their use of AI throughout the research lifecycle.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow research-guidance claim supported by official USask research AI guidance.

artifacts.claimText: USask administrative AI guidance strongly recommends not inputting personal information or confidential data into AI tools and says administrators should prioritize USask-approved AI tools.

artifacts.claimText: USask academic-integrity guidance says GenAI detection tools are not reliable and that no detection tool has been approved for use at the University of Saskatchewan.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: GenAI - Academic Integrity
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Tools to detect text or other outputs produced by GenAI are not reliable. False accusations can be devastating. No detection tool has been approved for use at the University of Saskatchewan.

artifacts.claimText: USask LTE Toolkit guidance says instructors planning student GenAI use should use approved or reviewed tools when accounts are required and says using GenAI to determine final grades is strongly discouraged.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: GenAI overview - LTE Toolkit
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If students are required to create an account, please use a tool that is listed in the A-Z Tool List as Approved for Academic Use or work through the process to Request a new LTE tool prior to any student use. Using GenAI to determine students’ final grades is strongly discouraged.

artifacts.claimText: A USask-hosted CGPS draft framework says graduate programs should determine whether and how generative AI may be used by graduate students and incorporate permitted/not-permitted use information into program guidelines.

artifacts.sourceTitle: CGPS Framework for Graduate Programs on the Use of Generative AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: CGPS Council deems it the responsibility of programs to determine whether and how generative AI may be used by graduate students and that CGPS Faculty Council instructs graduate programs to incorporate information on the types of generative AI that are permitted or not permitted.

artifacts.title: University of Saskatchewan AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found central University of Saskatchewan AI principles and role-specific AI guidelines, official academic-integrity GenAI guidance, LTE Toolkit guidance for teaching and learning tools, and a CGPS draft framework for graduate-program GenAI guidance. Claims remain source-scoped and agent-reviewed only.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The TeamDynamix KB candidate for submitting content to AI tools was login-gated or SSO-protected during this run and was not used as evidence.
The Computer Science graduate policy is official but department-scoped and was not used for university-wide claims.
The CGPS framework PDF is marked Draft November 21, 2023, so its extracted claim is kept as source-status context rather than a binding policy assertion.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
